_comment: Devil's Playground tuning data. Every numerical value in the game lives here. Modify and re-run tests to re-tune. Test_P1Tuning_ValuesInBand enforces sanity ranges.

possession._comment: Core possession loop parameters. Life timer is THE defining number of the game.
possession._comment_life_timer: Was 30.0 (MVP-original) -> 45.0 -> 60.0. The 8-personality x 10-seed matrix on 2026-05-21 measured ~4% win rate with 30s, ~12% with 45s. The balance target is: every personality wins some games AND loses some games, and every level is winnable by at least one personality. Round-2 lift to 60s (the new max) -- 45s landed 5 of 10 seeds with no winner, all of which were priest-dense layouts where the bot couldn't complete the 5-delivery loop before life ran out + priest closed in. 60s doubles MVP-baseline; combined with the priest perception/speed tuning round-2 changes below, targets ~50% overall win rate.

anchor_initial_position._comment: Demon's first anchor for the MVP level. Set to the GameLevel scene's standing-stones location.

possession._comment_scent_per_possession: Only applies on SUCCESSFUL possession. Failed attempts (cooldown-blocked, out-of-range, channel-interrupted) produce no scent.
possession._comment_scent_max: Saturation cap. GDD §4.5 states the range 0.0-1.0; values above this cap are clamped.

movement._comment_walk_speed: Speed used when walk-quiet (Ctrl) is held. Was 4.0 -> 6.0 -> 7.0. Round-2 of the 2026-05-21 game-balance pass bumped to 7.0 (0.875x jog_speed_mps). Round-1 at 6.0 still left Stealth at 0% win rate even with the wider life timer -- noise from forge/chest/door interactions still catches Stealth even with quiet footsteps. 7.0 puts walk-quiet within 1 m/s of jog, so the speed cost is small enough that the noise benefit can dominate on layouts with priest near interactables.
movement._comment_sprint_life_cost: Was 3.0 -> 1.5 -> 1.0 -> 1.5. The 2026-05-22 balance pass returned this to 1.5 after the 1.0 trial showed Speedrunner consistently winning 100% of cells (the cheap sprint let it outrun every layout). 1.5 means sprint costs 150% extra life-drain over base 1.0/s -- enough to deny Speedrunner the trivial sprint-everywhere strategy while still being cheaper than the original 3.0 that made sprint outright net-negative. Test_P1Sprint_DrainsLifeFaster's fMinDiff is 0.7, so the test still passes (sprint diff ~= 1.5 > 0.7 floor).
movement._comment_walk_loudness: Multiplier applied to THE MOVING VILLAGER'S FOOTSTEP LOUDNESS when walking (0.25 = quarter loudness). Was 0.5 -- round-2 of the 2026-05-21 game-balance pass dropped to 0.25 so walk-quiet has a meaningful acoustic advantage even when interactions (forge/chest/door) emit unrelated noise. Aelfric's perception radius is unchanged; the quieter footstep simply reaches him from a quarter as far away. NOT a reduction on Aelfric's hearing range.
movement._comment_footstep: MVP-1.7.5: baseline footstep emission tuning. Possessed villagers emit a footstep every footstep_interval_s while moving. Per Zenith perception default (loudness 0.3, radius 10m) -- matches CLAUDE.md's 'common sound types' table. walk_footstep_loudness_multiplier above is applied on top when Ctrl is held.

camera._comment_zoom: GDD §4.2 specifies PC zoom 30-80m, console 35-60m. MVP is PC-only.
camera._comment_follow: Auto-follow is OFF by default; players need to scan ahead.

priest._comment: Single canonical Aelfric for MVP. Variants come post-MVP — see _post_mvp_variants section at bottom of this file. NOTE: existing Priest_Behaviour.h currently HARDCODES sight=20, hearing=25, FOV=120; MVP-0.1.4 (priest tuning migration) must replace those with reads from this config.
priest._comment_sight_range: Was 25.0 -> 18.0 -> 15.0. Round-1 of the 2026-05-21 balance pass dropped to 18; round-2 dropped to 15 because 5 of 10 procgen seeds still had no winner. 15m means the priest can't see across most of a building footprint, so navigating between buildings gives the bot a real escape mechanic.
priest._comment_sight_fov: Was 110° -- narrowing to 90° opens up the 'circle around the priest' tactic on procgen layouts where the priest is centrally placed. Combined with the smaller sight_range, the priest is now noticeably more avoidable on open terrain.
priest._comment_peripheral: GDD §4.5 specifies 130° peripheral cone. Implementation uses this exactly.
priest._comment_hearing_range: Was 35.0 -- the 8p x 10s matrix on 2026-05-21 showed Stealth (lowest noise emission) still 0% win rate, suggesting hearing-range dominated detection on procgen. 25m matches the new sight range and gives walk-quiet a real escape window.
priest._comment_hearing_fog: Hearing range doubles when fog is dense (sound carries further when sight is short).
priest._comment_pursue_speed: Was 7.0 -> 5.5 -> 4.5. Round-2 of the 2026-05-21 balance pass dropped further so the priest now jogs at 4.5 vs villager jog at 8 -- a clear speed gap that lets the villager out-run the priest given a head start, instead of just delaying the catch.
priest._comment_apprehend_channel: Was 3.0 -> 4.5 -> 6.0. Round-2 of the 2026-05-21 balance pass widened to 6s -- with the slower pursue_speed_mps the priest still wins corners but the channel duration now gives the player a real beat to react with G-drop + click-switch.

fog_of_war._comment_possessed: Increased from prototype's 3.0 m for top-down legibility.
fog_of_war._comment_memory_states: Tiles have 4 states: NEVER_SEEN (full grey, default), VISITED_VISIBLE (tile last seen <=memory_visible_s ago), VISITED_DIM (last seen between memory_visible_s and memory_dim_s ago), VISITED_HIDDEN (last seen >memory_dim_s ago, returns to grey). A tile in NEVER_SEEN is distinct from a tile in VISITED_HIDDEN: NEVER_SEEN tiles can be exposed by FIRST visit; VISITED_HIDDEN tiles are 'forgotten' visited tiles. The shader treats both as grey but the test (Test_P2Fog_MemoryDimsAfter10s) distinguishes them via the never_seen flag in the tile buffer.

night._comment_dawn: MVP uses a 10-minute Night (down from GDD §5.1's 25-35 min) for fast-iteration playtesting. The shorter timer is INTENTIONAL FOR MVP and is restored toward GDD's range in post-MVP tuning.
night._comment_reagents_required: Was 5 (all-of-5) -- the 8p x 10s matrix on 2026-05-21 measured 5 procgen seeds where no bot personality could complete 5 deliveries within the test frame budget. Dropped to 3 (3-of-5) to satisfy the ratified balance criterion 'every level winnable by at least one personality'. The win condition is now popcount(collected_mask) >= 3 rather than mask == 0b11111 -- see DP_Win::NotifyObjectiveCollected in PublicInterfaces.cpp.
night._comment_modifiers: All escalation modifiers disabled for MVP (-1 = never). Re-enable post-MVP.

visual_telegraphs._comment: Hardcoded visual constants for the life-timer telegraphs (GDD §4.7).

test_constants._comment: Values used ONLY by automated tests. Production gameplay never reads these.

_post_mvp_variants._comment: Pre-shaped data for post-MVP features. Not read by any code in MVP. Promoted to top-level sections (priest_variants, hounds, etc.) when implementation lands.

priest_variants._comment: GDD §5.5: three Aelfric variants. Modifiers applied on top of base priest config.
